reason: Changing /etc/shadow permissions to the STIG‑specified mode is a standard hardening step that does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services. The fix merely restricts access to credential hashes and is unlikely to cause lockout because it does not modify user accounts or authentication mechanisms beyond file mode.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Improper permissions could allow non‑privileged users to read password hashes; If permissions are set too permissively, it violates the STIG
